tadasana.description: The foundation of all standing poses. Grounds scattered Vata energy by activating Apana Vata — the downward, stabilizing force.
tadasana.how_it_helps: Standing tall with conscious breath activates the parasympathetic nervous system, directly calming Vata's tendency toward anxiety, restlessness, and scattered thought.
steps: Stand with feet together, big toes touching
Distribute weight evenly across all four corners of both feet
Engage your thighs, lifting the kneecaps without locking the knees
Lengthen your tailbone toward the floor
Lift your chest and roll shoulders back and down
Arms alongside body, palms facing forward
Gaze forward, breathe deeply for 60 seconds

spine_vertical_angle.too_low: Stand taller — lengthen your spine upward
spine_vertical_angle.too_high: Soften slightly — avoid arching the lower back

left_hip_angle.too_high: Ground your hips, avoid tilting forward

vrikshasana.description: Single-leg balance that demands and builds the mental focus Vata types need most.
vrikshasana.how_it_helps: The challenge of balance forces the scattered Vata mind to become present. Consistent practice builds the stability and groundedness that is Vata's fundamental need.
steps: Begin in Tadasana
Shift weight to your left foot
Place right foot on inner left thigh (not on knee joint)
Press foot and thigh against each other
Bring palms together at heart center
Fix your gaze on a single point in front of you
Hold for 45 seconds, switch sides

spine_vertical_angle.too_low: Stand tall — do not lean to the side

standing_knee_angle.too_high: Avoid locking the knee — keep a micro bend

raised_hip_angle.too_low: Open your raised hip further outward

balasana.description: The ultimate surrender pose. Suppresses upward Vata movement and induces deep parasympathetic calm.
balasana.how_it_helps: The forward fold and fetal position directly activate the vagus nerve, dropping cortisol and calming the overactive nervous system that characterizes Vata imbalance.
steps: Kneel on the floor, big toes touching
Sit back on your heels
Separate knees hip-width apart
Exhale and fold forward, laying torso between thighs
Extend arms forward, palms down
Rest forehead on the mat
Breathe deeply for 90 seconds

hip_angle.too_low: Sit back further into your heels

spine_horizontal_angle.too_low: Lower your chest toward the mat
spine_horizontal_angle.too_high: Relax forward — let gravity pull you down

paschimottanasana.description: Forward compression cools the solar plexus — the seat of Pitta fire.
paschimottanasana.how_it_helps: The fold compresses the abdomen, stimulating the liver and cooling the digestive Agni that Pitta over-fires. The surrender required directly challenges Pitta's need for control.
steps: Sit on the floor with legs extended straight
Flex your feet, pressing heels forward
Inhale and lengthen your spine
Exhale and hinge forward from the hips (not the waist)
Reach for your feet, shins, or ankles — wherever you reach without rounding the spine
Keep the spine long, not rounded
Hold for 60 seconds, breathe into the stretch

hip_fold_angle.too_low: Hinge deeper from the hips — not the lower back
hip_fold_angle.too_high: You are not folding enough — exhale and release forward

ardha_chandrasana.description: Lateral expansion channels the Pitta force outward and upward, dispersing heat from the core.
ardha_chandrasana.how_it_helps: The full body extension with one-leg balance demands Pitta's full attention, but the lateral opening disperses the intensity. The moon (Chandra) energy in this pose is the antidote to Pitta's solar nature.
steps: Begin in Trikonasana on your right side
Bend right knee slightly, shift weight onto right foot
Place right fingertips on the floor 30cm ahead
Lift left leg parallel to the floor
Open left hip to stack on right hip
Extend left arm straight up
Turn gaze upward if balance allows
Hold 30 seconds, switch sides

standing_hip_angle.too_low: Open your hip more — rotate it toward the ceiling

raised_leg_hip_angle.too_low: Lift the raised leg higher — parallel to floor
raised_leg_hip_angle.too_high: Lower the raised leg to parallel

spine_angle.too_low: Open your chest — rotate torso toward ceiling
spine_angle.too_high: Keep spine neutral, do not arch

shavasana.description: Complete surrender. The hardest pose for Pitta — and the most necessary.
shavasana.how_it_helps: Total physical stillness forces the Pitta mind to stop achieving, controlling, and doing. Five minutes of real Shavasana lowers cortisol, drops blood pressure, and resets the entire Pitta stress cycle.
steps: Lie flat on your back
Feet fall naturally apart, 45 degrees outward
Arms at sides, palms facing up
Close your eyes
Let every muscle release — start from feet, move upward
Breathe naturally — no control
Stay completely still for 5 minutes

spine_flat_angle.too_low: Flatten your lower back toward the mat
spine_flat_angle.too_high: Relax — let the spine release naturally

shavasana.note: Shavasana pose check is minimal — primary feedback is about stillness, not angles. If too much movement is detected, speak: 'Be still — let your body completely release'

utkatasana.description: The pose that demands everything from Kapha. Generates intense internal heat to burn through stagnation.
utkatasana.how_it_helps: The sustained isometric hold fires the quadriceps and glutes — the largest muscles in the body. This generates maximum metabolic heat (Agni), directly addressing Kapha's cold, slow metabolism. The discomfort IS the medicine.
steps: Stand in Tadasana
Raise arms overhead, palms facing each other
Exhale and bend knees as if sitting into a chair
Thighs as parallel to floor as possible
Keep chest lifted, do not round the spine
Knees stay behind toes
Hold for 45 seconds — resist the urge to come up early

left_knee_angle.too_low: Sit lower — bend your knees more toward 90 degrees
left_knee_angle.too_high: Sit deeper — your thighs should be parallel to the floor

right_knee_angle.too_high: Hold the position — do not come up yet

spine_angle.too_low: Lift your chest — do not fold forward
spine_angle.too_high: Slight forward lean is needed — hinge from hips

left_shoulder_angle.too_low: Raise both arms higher — fully overhead

bhujangasana.description: Chest-opening backbend that stimulates the adrenals and breaks Kapha's characteristic forward slump.
bhujangasana.how_it_helps: The extension of the spine stimulates the adrenal glands (seated above kidneys, directly accessible in this pose), firing up Kapha's sluggish system. The chest opening counteracts the Kapha tendency toward depression and hunched posture.
steps: Lie face down, legs extended, toes pointing back
Place hands under shoulders, elbows close to body
Press tops of feet into the mat
Inhale and press through palms, lifting chest off the floor
Keep elbows slightly bent — this is not a full arm extension
Roll shoulders back, open chest fully
Gaze forward or slightly upward
Hold 30 seconds

spine_extension_angle.too_low: Lift your chest higher — open through the sternum
spine_extension_angle.too_high: Lower slightly — protect your lower back

left_elbow_angle.too_low: Do not fully straighten elbows — keep a gentle bend
left_elbow_angle.too_high: Bend elbows more — draw them toward your ribs

hip_angle.too_low: Keep hips pressed into the mat
hip_angle.too_high: Relax hips — do not tense the glutes

trikonasana.description: Full lateral stretch that opens every channel of the body, breaking Kapha's physical and energetic stagnation.
trikonasana.how_it_helps: The wide-leg stance and full lateral extension stimulates the lymphatic system running along the torso, breaking Kapha's tendency toward fluid retention and energetic sluggishness. The twist opens the liver and spleen — both Kapha-accumulating organs.
steps: Stand with feet 3.5 to 4 feet apart
Turn right foot out 90 degrees, left foot in 15 degrees
Extend arms out to the sides, parallel to floor
Exhale and extend torso over right leg
Lower right hand to shin, ankle, or the floor
Left arm extends straight up
Turn gaze up toward the raised hand
Hold 45 seconds, switch sides

left_hip_angle.too_low: Open your left hip — rotate toward the ceiling
left_hip_angle.too_high: Square your hips more toward the front

left_knee_angle.too_high: Keep the knee straight — do not bend

right_knee_angle.too_low: Straighten your right knee — do not bend it
right_knee_angle.too_high: Right leg must stay straight in Triangle

spine_lateral_angle.too_low: Lean further sideways over your right leg
spine_lateral_angle.too_high: You are folding too far — maintain the triangle shape

upper_arm_angle.too_low: Raise your top arm fully — straight to the ceiling
